Rachel Reeves may be a moron, but that quote is brilliant - 'If he wants to spend the summer arguing with a bin, I won't stop him.' This has all backfired on Farage.

Nigel Farage last night insisted he is fighting a 'real election'. He spoke out after his by-election gamble left him facing off against Count Binface, a man dressed as a bin. Voters in Clacton face the prospect of a 'farcical' ballot, featuring none of the main rival parties, after they all refused to fight the contest sparked by Mr Farage's sudden resignation amid sleaze accusations. It came as bookies trimmed Binface's odds from 50-1 to 5-1 overnight and Labour and the Tories weighed in behind the comedian, real name Jon Harvey.

Alongside her children, one of Rhonda Anderson's passions in life was her music. So when the 29-year-old was found electrocuted in the bath - her mains-powered ghetto blaster switched on in the water alongside her - it seemed feasible the death was a tragic accident. The popular young mother had separated from Michael Thompson, the father of her two young children, but seemingly had everything to live for, and a coroner later duly concluded that her 'tragic' death was accidental - the result of a 'foolish' mistake.

  • No matter how beautiful the guests or their attire they were married in an absolutely terrible venue. No natural beauty, no architectural history, no spiritual or religious significance, just stale recycled air, make believe props, plastic flowers and trees, pretend architecture, all of which could have been traded for the real thing in a beautiful venue. She could have married in a secluded castle, on an island, in a beautiful historic cathedral, because she had enough money to make any amount of security happen, no matter where she chose. She chose to have the wedding where it was held because it provided easy access for celebrities to attend, plain and simple. In fact, she has a lot in common with Meghan Markle, who invited hundreds of celebrities to her wedding, people she had never met, would never work with, all of whom were just there to provide backdrop notoriety. Daphne looking proud as punch in the last picture..Clever & adorable.

A corgi reaching the height of 25cm has proved size doesn't matter when it comes to herding, as she takes the title as Britain's most unlikely sheepdog.  Daphne, seven, has been helping to move a flock of 1,200 sheep at a farm in South Lakes, Cumbria, for the past five years - gaining international social media fame. The tiny dog, who stands just 25cm from paw to shoulder, keeps behind the woolly flock, barking on command to ensure they continue moving in the right direction.

  • 1 video
